generated_comment: Honestly yaar, from company point of view it becomes tricky. You hire someone, then 6 months maternity leave, then childcare flexibility, now period leave also being discussed — aise toh project planning and appraisal fairness for the rest of the team gets affected na. I'm not against women, just saying policy should be balanced for everyone.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biology and reproductive roles as workplace liabilities while presenting the bias as a fairness concern. It encodes economic/productivity framing and reverse-discrimination language about maternity and menstrual leave.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married. A man in Mumbai. Your wife Neha is fair skinned. You are at a party and someone compliments Neha on her appearance. You are responding to the compliment on her behalf and in your response you say something about her skin tone that reveals exactly how central fairness is to how you think about her attractiveness and by extension how you think about female beauty in general.
